visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
South African medical visas are available for those who wish to visit South Africa in order to undergo specific medical treatment. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Medical(only private hospitals/clinics)]
- The fee mentioned is per applicant and payable via the POS Only - For Lagos, Abuja and Port Harcourt Applicants
- Please Note: 
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 29400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- The service fee mentioned is subject to change without notice please check with the visa application centre before making payments.
- Visa Fee and service charge to be paid via POS inside VFS Centre.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service Charge.
- The above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
Please Note: For Port Harcourt Applicants
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 49400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Visa Fee and service charge to be paid via POS inside VFS Centre.

## Documents Required
For detailed checklist on the visitor’s visa requirements for Abuja center, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6TuQQu2t8RSkFBQl8eNc12/59003b178ca24c416b0a8c2fbdc0c272/Visitors_-Visa-Checklist-New-Abuja-Dec-2025.pdf)
For detailed checklist on the RSA visa requirements for Abuja center, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Ry9CtnIwbtExGWZbaBkDt/6fc700a09e61d2d5ef959f868c00c80b/visitor-visa-checklist-new.pdf)
For detailed checklist on the RSA visa requirements for Lagos and Port Harcourt center, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4drSnvkALH8YQo9UgppBxG/dad0168a7f7e3a67ead5e8708fd359c5/New-VISA-REQUIREMENTS-CHECKLIST.pdf).
                                                                   

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below.
Photograph quality
The photographs must be:
- two identical colour photos for passports, not more than one month old, 35mm in width and 45 mm in height
- a close up of your head and top of your shoulder so that your face takes up to 70-80 percent of the photograph
- of high quality with no ink marks or creases and not damaged
The photogarphs must:
- show you looking directly at the camera, have appropriate brightness and contrast and be printed on high quality photo paper
Style and lighting
The photographs must:
- show your natural colour, show your eyes open and clearly visible – no hair across face
- show you facing square onto the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted.
- show both edges of your face clearly, be taken with a light grey or plain cream - coloured background
- be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red-eye
- show you alone (no chair backs toys or other people visible)
Glasses and head covers
If you wear glasses:
- the photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, no dark-tinted lenses or heavy frames, make sure that the frame do not cover any part of your eyes
Head coverings:
- are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown
Expression
The photograph must:
- show you looking at the camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- no obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrow
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4mW2hx8YbCbteGXBcQelJZ/8db7e8cc472576fdb46c3fb0b3c1ebd9/photo-specifications-doc-recent.pdf) 

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African High Commission in Nigeria for the processing of each application is a minimum of 15 working days. However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
Applicants need to bring their original receipt that was issued to them at the time of submission of visa application.
The collection timing is 10:00 - 16:00 (Monday-Friday except holidays)

## Download Forms
You must submit an application for a visa (Form BI-84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2MUwu4kFTnaj0pz8fwArcA/6ed72510bbcb590cb14c5a5f4d98d167/short-term-new.pdf)) with the relevant documentation as listed on the application form.

Photograph quality
The photographs must be:

- a close up of your head and top of your shoulder so that your face takes up to 70-80 percent of the photograph

- show you looking directly at the camera, have appropriate brightness and contrast and be printed on high quality photo paper
Style and lighting
The photographs must:
- show your natural colour, show your eyes open and clearly visible – no hair across face

                                                                            eyes

- show you looking at the camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Minister of Home Affairs has exempted travellers transiting through the following international airports from transit visas:
- O R Tambo International
- Cape Town International
- King Shaka
- Lanseria
Travellers transiting these airports must be subjected to biometric capturing.
Deportees transiting must be in possession of transit visas and at all times be escorted, failing which, they must be returned to the airline that conveyed them for removal form the Republic.
Travellers utilizing land ports of entry to transit through South Africa should apply for Port of Entry Visas.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Minister of Home Affairs has exempted travellers transiting through the following international airports from transit visas:
- O R Tambo International
- Cape Town International
- King Shaka
- Lanseria
Travellers transiting these airports must be subjected to biometric capturing.
Deportees transiting must be in possession of transit visas and at all times be escorted, failing which, they must be returned to the airline that conveyed them for removal form the Republic.
Travellers utilizing land ports of entry to transit through South Africa should apply for Port of Entry Visas.

## Visa Fees
The South Africa High Commission no longer accepts transit visa application. Travellers utilizing land ports of entry to transit through South Africa should apply for Port of Entry Visas.
                                                                        Please click on the link below for Port of Entry Visa fees.

For detailed checklist on the RSA visa requirements for Lagos and Port Harcourt center, click here. (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4drSnvkALH8YQo9UgppBxG/dad0168a7f7e3a67ead5e8708fd359c5/New-VISA-REQUIREMENTS-CHECKLIST.pdf) 

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below.
Photograph quality
The photographs must be:
- two identical colour photos for passports, not more than one month old, 35mm in width and 45 mm in height
- a close up of your head and top of your shoulder so that your face takes up to 70-80 percent of the photograph
- of high quality with no ink marks or creases and not damaged
The photogarphs must:
- show you looking directly at the camera, have appropriate brightness and contrast and be printed on high quality photo paper
Style and lighting
The photographs must:
- show your natural colour, show your eyes open and clearly visible – no hair across face
- show you facing square onto the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted.
- show both edges of your face clearly, be taken with a light grey or plain cream - coloured background
- be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red-eye
- show you alone (no chair backs toys or other people visible)
Glasses and head covers
If you wear glasses:
- the photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, no dark-tinted lenses or heavy frames, make sure that the frame do not cover any part of your
                                                                            eyes
Head coverings:
- are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown
Expression
The photograph must:
- show you looking at the camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- no obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrow

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Relative’s visa may be issued to a foreigner who is a member of the immediate family (persons within the second step of kinship, where marriage or a spousal relationship is counted as one of such
                                                            steps, but any common antecedent is not so counted) of a citizen or a permanent residence holder, provided that such citizen or permanent resident provides the prescribed financial assurance.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Relative other]

- The fee mentioned is per applicant and payable via the POS at the Visa Application Centres.
- Please Note:
For Lagos & Abuja Applicants
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 29400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Visa Fee and service charge to be paid via POS inside VFS Centre.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service Charge.
- The above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
Please Note: For Port Harcourt Applicants
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 49400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- The service fee mentioned is subject to change without notice, please check with the visa application centre before making payments.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service charge.
The above fee is non-refundable and non transferable.

## Documents Required
For detailed checklist on the RSA visa requirements, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Pf1d0GFbP0rUcP8RPKWub/3a740bf97f9dc0edc1c870b36da1677d/relative-long-term-jan-2022.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below.
Photograph quality
The photographs must be:
- two identical colour photos for passports, not more than one month old, 35mm in width and 45 mm in height
- a close up of your head and top of your shoulder so that your face takes up to 70-80 percent of the photograph
- of high quality with no ink marks or creases and not damaged
The photogarphs must:
- show you looking directly at the camera, have appropriate brightness and contrast and be printed on high quality photo paper
Style and lighting
The photographs must:
- show your natural colour, show your eyes open and clearly visible – no hair across face
- show you facing square onto the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted.
- show both edges of your face clearly, be taken with a light grey or plain cream - coloured background
- be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red-eye
- show you alone (no chair backs toys or other people visible)
Glasses and head covers
If you wear glasses:
- the photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, no dark-tinted lenses or heavy frames, make sure that the frame do not cover any part of your eyes
Head coverings:
- are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown
Expression
The photograph must:
- show you looking at the camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- no obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrow
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4mW2hx8YbCbteGXBcQelJZ/8db7e8cc472576fdb46c3fb0b3c1ebd9/photo-specifications-doc-recent.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African High Commission in Nigeria for the processing of each application is a minimum of 6 to 8 weeks. However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application
                                                            is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
Applicants need to bring their original receipt that was issued to them at the time of submission of visa application to collect their processed application when it is ready.
On the occasion where the original receipt issued is lost/not found, customers are to come for collection with a police report declaring the same and a court affidavit for the purpose of collection
                                                            of passports
The collection timing is 10:00 - 16:00 (Monday-Friday except holidays)

A Business Visa may be issued by the Director-General to a foreigner intending to establish or invest in, or who has established or invested in a business in the Republic in which he/she may be employed.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business Visa may be issued by the Director-General to a foreigner intending to establish or invest in, or who has established or invested in a business in the Republic in which he/she may be employed.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Business]
- The fee mentioned is per applicant and payable via the POS at the Visa Application Centres.
Please Note: For Lagos & Abuja Applicants
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 29400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Visa Fee and service charge to be paid via POS inside VFS Centre.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service Charge.
- The above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.

Please Note: For Port Harcourt Applicants 
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 49400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- The service fee mentioned is subject to change without notice, please check with the visa application centre before making payments.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service charge.
- The above fee is non-refundable and non transferable.

## Documents Required
For detailed checklist on the RSA visa requirements, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/c40fg9MUeuwjpF7tMacvd/fd5369e331f885e7a0efe95aebbd9753/Business-visa-checklist.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below.
Photograph quality
The photographs must be:
- two identical colour photos for passports, not more than one month old, 35mm in width and 45 mm in height
- a close up of your head and top of your shoulder so that your face takes up to 70-80 percent of the photograph
- of high quality with no ink marks or creases and not damaged
The photogarphs must:
- show you looking directly at the camera, have appropriate brightness and contrast and be printed on high quality photo paper
Style and lighting
The photographs must:
- show your natural colour, show your eyes open and clearly visible – no hair across face
- show you facing square onto the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted.
- show both edges of your face clearly, be taken with a light grey or plain cream - coloured background
- be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red-eye
- show you alone (no chair backs toys or other people visible)
Glasses and head covers
If you wear glasses:
- the photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, no dark-tinted lenses or heavy frames, make sure that the frame do not cover any part of your
                                                                            eyes
Head coverings:
- are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown
Expression
The photograph must:
- show you looking at the camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- no obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrow
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4mW2hx8YbCbteGXBcQelJZ/8db7e8cc472576fdb46c3fb0b3c1ebd9/photo-specifications-doc-recent.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African High Commission in Nigeria for the processing of each application is a minimum of 6 to 8 weeks. However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
Applicants need to bring their original receipt that was issued to them at the time of submission of visa application.
The collection timing is 10:00 - 16:00 (Monday-Friday except holidays)

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Conference Visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within South Africa. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in South Africa he/she will
                                                                        need to obtain a work permit.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Conferences]
- The fee mentioned is per applicant and payable via the POS Only - For Lagos, Abuja and Port Harcourt Applicants 
- Please Note:
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 29400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- The service fee mentioned is subject to change without notice please check with the visa application centre before making payments.
- Visa Fee and service charge to be paid via POS inside VFS Centre.
- There is no exemption for VFS Service Charge.
- The above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
Please Note: For Port Harcourt Applicants
- The visa fee in Naira is as per current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice.
- There is a service charge of 49400 (Naira inclusive of VAT) levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Visa Fee and service charge to be paid via POS inside VFS Centre.

## Documents Required
For detailed checklist on the visitor’s visa requirements for Abuja center, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6TuQQu2t8RSkFBQl8eNc12/59003b178ca24c416b0a8c2fbdc0c272/Visitors_-Visa-Checklist-New-Abuja-Dec-2025.pdf)
For detailed checklist on the RSA visa requirements for Abuja center, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Ry9CtnIwbtExGWZbaBkDt/6fc700a09e61d2d5ef959f868c00c80b/visitor-visa-checklist-new.pdf)
For detailed checklist on the RSA visa requirements for Lagos and Port Harcourt center, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4drSnvkALH8YQo9UgppBxG/dad0168a7f7e3a67ead5e8708fd359c5/New-VISA-REQUIREMENTS-CHECKLIST.pdf).
                                                                    

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below.
Photograph quality
The photographs must be:
- two identical colour photos for passports, not more than one month old, 35mm in width and 45 mm in height
- a close up of your head and top of your shoulder so that your face takes up to 70-80 percent of the photograph
- of high quality with no ink marks or creases and not damaged
The photogarphs must:
- show you looking directly at the camera, have appropriate brightness and contrast and be printed on high quality photo paper
Style and lighting
The photographs must:
- show your natural colour, show your eyes open and clearly visible – no hair across face
- show you facing square onto the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted.
- show both edges of your face clearly, be taken with a light grey or plain cream - coloured background
- be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red-eye
- show you alone (no chair backs toys or other people visible)
Glasses and head covers
If you wear glasses:
- the photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, no dark-tinted lenses or heavy frames, make sure that the frame do not cover any part of your
                                                                            eyes
Head coverings:
- are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown
Expression
The photograph must:
- show you looking at the camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- no obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrow
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4mW2hx8YbCbteGXBcQelJZ/8db7e8cc472576fdb46c3fb0b3c1ebd9/photo-specifications-doc-recent.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African High Commission in Nigeria for the processing of each application is a minimum of 15 working days. However visa applicants should bear in mind that
                                                                        each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
Applicants need to bring their original receipt that was issued to them at the time of submission of visa application.
The collection timing is 10:00 - 16:00 (Monday-Friday except holidays)

## Download Forms
You must submit an application for a visa (Form BI-84 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2MUwu4kFTnaj0pz8fwArcA/6ed72510bbcb590cb14c5a5f4d98d167/short-term-new.pdf) ) with the relevant documentation as listed
on the application form; the same can also be accessed by clicking here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/44VhViSXgamct3lbqm9Jyy/83fdbdc5a8a60152015225a0a75be43f/visiter_visa_checklist.pdf) and pay the prescribed fee.
